Title: Overexpression of REDUCED WALL ACETYLATION C increases xylan acetylation and biomass recalcitrance in Populus

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: REDUCED WALL ACETYLATION (RWA) family protein; Cas1_AcylT motif; 11 transmembrane helices
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in stem differentiating xylem and secondary cell wall-forming xylem; also relatively high in phloem, cambium, and expanding xylem; induced by PtrSND1-B1 in SDX protoplasts
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in stems of transgenic Populus deltoides lines
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: secondary cell walls of stem/xylem woody biomass
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall structural property affecting biomass processing and saccharification efficiency
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases xylan acetylation, increases lignin content and S/G ratio, and reduces saccharification efficiency/sugar release
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Activation tagging mutant identification; overexpression in Populus deltoides; RT-qPCR; Golgi colocalization in protoplasts; acetyl quantification of DMSO-extracted xylan; pyMBMS lignin analysis; saccharification assay; coexpression and eQTL analyses
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth at 25 °C under 16/8 h light/dark photoperiod; biomass pretreatment for saccharification at 180 °C
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HRD; SND2; NST1; VND1; VND4; MYB46; MYB50; MYB61; MYB103
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/SRA: SRP138581; SRP138583; SRP138623-26; SRP138628; SRP138630; SRP138641; SRP183923; SRP183947-49; SRP183954; SRP183990; SRP184008-09; SRP184021; eQTL using xylem transcriptome of 444 accessions and resequencing variants from 973 accessions; BioProjects PRJNA320431 and PRJNA215318
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Activation tagging and overexpression demonstrated that PtRWA-C positively controls xylan acetylation in poplar wood. 2. Increased PtRWA-C expression elevated lignin content and syringyl/guaiacyl ratio and decreased glucose/xylose release, indicating higher biomass recalcitrance. 3. PtRWA-C is Golgi localized and likely mediates acetyl-group translocation to support xylan O-acetylation. 4. PtRWA-C expression is directly regulated by secondary cell wall TFs and HRD through promoter cis-elements including a cis-eQTL-containing AP2/XYLAT site.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced saccharification efficiency and lower sugar release; increased biomass recalcitrance
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for reducing or fine-tuning wood recalcitrance via gene or promoter editing; manipulating PtRWA-C expression/cis-elements could alter acetylation patterns for biofuel and biomaterial applications
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because the study supports a transport/translocation role in O-acetyl donor movement rather than direct lumenal catalytic acetyl transfer; overexpression caused no obvious growth penalty in tested lines

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: natural association panel for eQTL; promoter assays also in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: xylan acetylation via transcriptional control of PtRWA-C
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates PtRWA-C expression and thereby promotes xylan acetylation-associated biomass recalcitrance
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PtRWA-C promoter; activation is stronger with the cis-eQTL C allele than mutant G allele
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation by direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally decisive upstream activator identified by trans-eQTL and validated as a direct regulator of PtRWA-C, linking natural regulatory variation to xylan acetylation control
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: eQTL mapping; yeast one-hybrid assay; promoter mutagenesis; dual-luciferase transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient dual-luciferase assay in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: eQTL using xylem transcriptome from 444 Populus trichocarpa accessions and resequencing variants from 973 accessions
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HRD colocalizes genetically with a strong trans-eQTL for PtRWA-C expression. 2. HRD directly binds the PtRWA-C promoter at fragment p3 containing the AP2/XYLAT cis-eQTL site. 3. Mutation of the cis-eQTL weakens HRD binding and transcriptional activation, demonstrating allele-dependent regulation of PtRWA-C.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potentially increasing HRD activity may elevate recalcitrance through higher xylan acetylation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for regulatory engineering or promoter-allele selection/editing to modulate PtRWA-C expression and wood processing traits
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper text contains one instance of 'HDR', but context and gene ID indicate HRD; applied species for metabolite effect is Populus, while transient reporter validation was performed in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PtRWA-C promoter fragments p3/p3m in reporter assays
